skill.name: billing-automation
skill.description: Build automated billing systems for recurring payments, invoicing, subscription lifecycle, and dunning management. Use when implementing subscription billing, automating invoicing, or managing recurring payment systems.
skill.summary: Design subscription billing, invoicing, dunning, proration, tax, and usage billing workflows.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, weak crypto, reconnaissance, and network patterns. Review found the command and blocker hits are false positives from Markdown fences and ordinary billing terms. The only confirmed concern is a hardcoded example billing URL with no request or exfiltration behavior.

low_findings.title: False positive: Markdown code fences flagged as shell execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings are Markdown triple backtick fences around documentation examples. No shell command execution, interpreter invocation, or user-controlled command path is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each flagged location is a Markdown code fence delimiter or nearby documentation boundary. The file contains examples, but no Ruby backtick execution syntax.

low_findings.title: False positive: Weak crypto terms are billing text
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings match substrings in normal words such as description or ordinary invoice field names. No cryptographic algorithm use was found at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines define billing descriptions, invoice line items, and display labels. There is no hash, cipher, key, or encryption operation.

low_findings.title: False positive: Identifier fields flagged as reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance findings point to generated billing identifiers and customer or subscription IDs. They do not enumerate host, network, user, or environment information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is subscription and invoice object construction. No commands or APIs collect system metadata.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded example billing URL
low_findings.description: The dunning email example contains a fixed https://app.example.com/billing URL. This is a placeholder and no network call is made, but implementers should replace it with a configured customer portal URL.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is present in an email template example and uses the reserved example.com domain. It is low risk because it is not used for data transfer by the skill.

content.value_statement: Recurring billing systems need reliable lifecycle, invoice, tax, and recovery logic. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code design practical subscription billing workflows.
actual_capabilities: Models subscription states from trial through active, past due, paused, and canceled.
Outlines recurring billing cycle processing with invoice creation and payment attempts.
Defines dunning retry schedules and customer payment failure notifications.
Explains proration calculations for plan changes and seat changes.
Shows tax calculation patterns for sales tax, VAT, and GST.
Covers invoice lifecycle steps and usage-based billing charge calculation.
limitations: Examples are instructional and require integration with real storage, payment, and email services.
Tax rates and compliance logic are simplified and must be verified for each jurisdiction.
The skill does not provide complete production security, audit logging, or PCI compliance controls.
Referenced assets and supporting files are described but not present in the scanned package.

use_cases.title: Launch SaaS subscriptions
use_cases.description: Design subscription states, billing cycles, trials, renewals, and customer payment flows.

use_cases.title: Improve failed payment recovery
use_cases.description: Create dunning schedules, retry rules, and customer notices for unpaid invoices.

use_cases.title: Add usage and proration billing
use_cases.description: Calculate mid-cycle plan changes, seat changes, and usage charges across billing periods.

prompt_templates.title: Map a billing lifecycle
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this billing automation skill to map the subscription lifecycle for a monthly SaaS plan with a free trial.

prompt_templates.title: Design invoice generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design invoice generation for subscriptions, taxes, line items, and payment status changes.

prompt_templates.title: Build dunning logic
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to draft a failed payment recovery workflow with retries, emails, grace periods, and cancellation rules.

prompt_templates.title: Review proration and usage billing
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review proration, seat changes, usage tiers, and edge cases for a production billing design.

output_examples.input: Plan a subscription billing flow for a monthly product with a 14 day trial.
output_examples.output: A lifecycle outline covering trial start, activation, invoice creation, payment attempt, period advancement, and failed payment handling.

output_examples.input: Create a dunning policy for failed card payments.
output_examples.output: A retry schedule with customer notices, payment recovery steps, grace period behavior, and cancellation after exhausted retries.

output_examples.input: Explain how to bill a customer who upgrades mid-cycle.
output_examples.output: A proration method that compares unused old plan value with remaining new plan value and returns the net charge or credit.

best_practices: Keep billing state transitions explicit and auditable.
Use configured payment portal links instead of hardcoded customer URLs.
Validate tax, retry, and cancellation rules with legal and finance owners.
anti_patterns: Do not treat the examples as complete production billing code.
Do not hardcode tax rates, plan IDs, or customer portal links in production.
Do not cancel accounts before clear notices and retry windows are defined.

faq.question: Does this skill process real payments?
faq.answer: No. It provides design guidance and example patterns for billing systems.

faq.question: Can it help with Stripe billing?
faq.answer: Yes. The examples include payment processor concepts that can guide a Stripe integration.

faq.question: Does it cover failed payment recovery?
faq.answer: Yes. It describes dunning retries, customer notices, recovery, and cancellation handling.

faq.question: Does it handle tax compliance completely?
faq.answer: No. The tax examples are simplified and must be reviewed for each jurisdiction.

faq.question: Can it support usage-based billing?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains usage tracking, tiered pricing, per-unit pricing, and volume pricing.

faq.question: Is this safe for marketplace publication?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found low risk and no confirmed malicious behavior.
